title: Could Rei come back from 3i
wikitext:
[]: Could Rei come back from 3i?             Quote Post by Duke » Fri Mar 06, 2009 8:30 pm It says all humans had the ability to return if they could imagine themselves. So, my question is, could Rei return from 3i? Even though she got like butchered up?

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Fri Mar 06, 2009 8:36 pm I would think not, given that Rei is a clone of Yui which somehow contained some vague portion of Lilith's soul and seemed to lack a soul of her own. Just my take on it, for what it's worth, and I'm likely to be waaay off...

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Mar 06, 2009 8:37 pm well she does in a way, shes seen floating above the sea of lcl, but its just quantum magic again. Shes also not human technicaly as her soul belongs to lilith. And if lilith can come back, the other angels could possibly be back

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 06, 2009 9:07 pm I get the feeling that Rei can't 'come back' so to speak because she's gone beyond such limitations. After 3I she seems to be no longer bound to a point in spacetime. She more than likely will make vague appearances from time to time, however she probably won't stick around for any actual interaction.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Mar 06, 2009 11:26 pm I'm sure she could if she wanted to. Rei can pretty much do whatever the hell she wants at this point.

[]:             Quote Post by henna » Sat Mar 07, 2009 1:49 am I think that for all intents and purposes, Rei has become Lilith's inheritor, taking whatever powers and privelidges that conferrs. She was essentially the container of her soul for a long period of time, and in all likelihood, it was simpler for Lilith to retain the Rei form as it now has so many life memories attatched to it.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sat Mar 07, 2009 5:47 am This may be offtopic, but Lilith dies completely with the Lilim amalgam, doesn't she? Wait, no she doesn't, you see Rei at the end. This post has no point.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Mar 07, 2009 12:03 pm FallenTabris wrote: This may be offtopic, but Lilith dies completely with the Lilim amalgam, doesn't she? Wait, no she doesn't, you see Rei at the end. This post has no point.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 07, 2009 1:36 pm Plus, there's the fact that Rei doesn't really exist anymore. She's almost certaily the dominant factor, but now she's just part of Rei1/Rei2/Rei3/Unit00/Kaworu/Lilith/Adam... and possibly all the other angels as well, depending on how one interprets the 'conference call' in ep24. She can't go back to being just herself. It's also possible that GNR fell apart because the soul merged with Yui in the 01 'ark'.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 07, 2009 8:21 pm NemZ wrote: It's also possible that GNR fell apart because the soul merged with Yui in the 01 'ark'.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Mar 07, 2009 9:23 pm she's just part of Rei1/Rei2/Rei3/Unit00/Kaworu/Lilith/Adam...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Mar 08, 2009 4:25 am Reichu wrote: Since we see Rei hanging out on Earth after Yui leaves, what is the above based on?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Mar 08, 2009 2:40 pm Though the soul is the same, I believe that each variation of Rei/Lilith/Zerogouki should be treated as an individual to some extent based upon their different experiences. Same for Adam/Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Mar 08, 2009 3:05 pm The point being that just as Rei 3 is not Rei 2, GNR can't go back to being just Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 08, 2009 3:14 pm "GNR", that huge white 12-winged thing, might be said to represent a fusion of powers more than anything else. The personas of Rei/Lilith, Kaworu/Adam, and Yui/Eva would appear to remain separate, however. The souls never "fuse" and lose their respective identities. They use three separate avatars and speak with three separate voices. Rei's reunion with her original self ("Welcome home") is what should be receiving the emphasis here.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Mar 08, 2009 9:46 pm And even so, it's not like Lilith ever did a whole lot; then there's the theory that Rei 1 had Lilith's memories, which meant that nothing would've actually been left behind in Lilith's body to merge with.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 08, 2009 10:23 pm I don't think we're in any position to make definitive statements about what Lilith did or did not do or what memories Rei 1 did or did not have. (Yes, you said "theory" and all, but you don't seem averse to giving it a lot of weight.) Whatever the case with memories and so forth (it's ultimately up to personal taste, I suppose), Rei 3 --> Lilith represents a significant vessel upgrade, and just that on its own is going to have major effects on the Rei-Lilith persona.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Mar 09, 2009 10:22 am Either way, whatever the hell directed Instrumentality seems to be dominantly Rei, since whatever she gained, Rei's thoughts and emotions are at the core of her personality; otherwise what Shinji wanted wouldn't be such a big deal. I don't personally believe Rei necessarily had to change that much, as it strikes me as unnecessary (lol) and ultimately meaningless.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Mar 09, 2009 4:06 pm Also, is it even possible for her TO die? She has quantum magics at one point, thus she exists everywhere and all the time, so since that body was destroyed, her current physical form would be gone, but she could probably very easily reform a body, using her quantum magics and an A.T. Field. Dont'cha think?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 09, 2009 5:00 pm To answer the thread's question: I'd say no since Lilith's physical body is actually shown falling apart in EoE. Next time we see [bookend] Rei, apparently it's just her soul instead of her body. Besides, I've read somewhere that Rei (and Kaworu) would be barred from returning ( although it's confusing why THEY couldn't return and just restore their bodies if everybody else is supposedly able to as well)...it was probably here on the forums somewhere.
